title: The Real Reasons Behind Skyrocketing CEO Pay
subtitle: CEO年薪凭什么是员工的几百倍？

paragraphs.en: CEO pay in major US firms has climbed sharply since the mid-1970s, and the gap with ordinary workers now looks dramatic. One explanation is not only bargaining power but scarcity of leaders who can run larger and faster global businesses. As top firms scale, the market price of proven executive talent rises with them.
paragraphs.zh: 自二十世纪七十年代中期以来，美国大型企业CEO的薪酬急剧攀升，与普通员工的差距如今十分悬殊。一种解释是，这不仅是议价权的问题，更在于能够驾驭规模更大、运转更快的全球业务的领导者本身就很稀缺。随着头部企业不断扩张，成熟高管人才的市场价格也随之水涨船高。

paragraphs.en: Modern CEOs face responsibilities that go beyond internal operations, including capital markets, geopolitical shocks, media pressure, and technology transitions. A small public mistake can wipe out brand value, so communication and crisis response now carry higher stakes. In many industries, the role is broader and less forgiving than it used to be.
paragraphs.zh: 当代CEO的职责已远超内部运营范畴，还需同时应对资本市场、地缘政治冲击、舆论压力和技术转型。一次小小的公开失误就可能抹去巨大的品牌价值，因此沟通与危机应对的风险比以往更高。在许多行业，这一职位的广度与容错率都远不如前。

paragraphs.en: Corporate governance has tightened in the same era, yet boards still compete aggressively for top candidates, especially outsiders. Investors also tend to reward compensation structures tied to long-term performance metrics. These patterns suggest high pay can reflect fierce talent competition, even if fairness concerns remain legitimate.
paragraphs.zh: 同一时期公司治理实际上更趋严格，但董事会仍在激烈争夺顶尖候选人，尤其是外部人才。投资者也往往更青睐与长期绩效指标挂钩的薪酬结构。这些现象表明，高薪可能确实反映了激烈的人才竞争，尽管人们对公平性的质疑依然合理。
